query: This new auth middleware works, but it feels too complex. Simplify it without changing behavior.
expected_behavior: Agent recognizes a natural-language simplify request and activates simplify
Agent routes to Mode B: Apply only when an active diff, recent scope, or explicit target passes the Apply Eligibility Gate; otherwise it falls back to Mode A: Analyze or asks before editing
Agent preserves the skill's clarity-without-behavior-change contract
Agent focuses on recently modified code by default
Agent avoids expanding into a generic review or architecture redesign
